Brent Marinan.review_text: Brent is an excellent teacher and gives quite a few extra credit opportunities. He also makes it readily apparent what will be on the test, so there is really no excuse to not succeed in this course.
Awesome professor. Favorite this semester. Class goes by quickly as always working on real world data assignments in groups. Replied so quickly to emails.

This professor was good and let us make the syllabus at the beginning of the semester, but he wasn't that great at teaching. I've never taken a stats class before, and he just read off the slides the entire time, and sometimes his slides were outdated and confusing. He did provide lots of extra credit, so he makes the class easy to pass.

Overall really nice and very understanding guy, but taking this at 8am was brutal. The lecture presentations are pretty vague and he doesn't give much detail, which leaves you to figure out how to do it on weekly group assignments. Honestly, take this for extra credit but you're better off going with any other BNAN 276 teacher.

Professor was super supportive. Many of the assignments were working together and interesting. Stats isn't the easiest but he made an early morning class much easier to get up for. Take him if you can.

Great Professor to take for BNAN 276. Would recommend him to everyone to take him. He offers extra credit left and right as long as you go in person. Great lectures, uses real-world examples. Even though I do not really enjoy business stats at all, he made it possible and easy to pass this class.
Brent was such a great professor! BNAD 276 was so easy with him and gives you many opportunities to help your grade.

Took class as a senior. Brent has been one of my favorite professors at U of A. I don't miss these large class sizes though. Took AP stats in HS and this class was at a much higher level. I don't have to take the next stat course, but hands down would take him again. Keep up with the work.

If you have to take a BNAD class Brent's the guy to do it with. He'll do anything to help his students learn & he's also super funny which makes a hard class a lil better. This class is challenging, but as long as you do ALL the practice problems he posts and go over the HW questions before a test it's manageable. He also gives some extra credit

Overall, the material was not hard, but what got me was the exams.  Majority of the class would get pretty good grades on the exams, but I didn't do well since I get nervous on them and definitely should have been more prepared.  500 Total Points: 400 points on exams (4 exams) 100 points on other things (homework/case studies/ attendance)
Had Brent for a stats class and a programming class. Explain concepts with realistic examples. Really cares about the students.

Best professor I could ever ask for tbh, tons of extra credits PLUS snacks on review days before the exam. Whoever rate this man below a 4 is def never in class because the exams and Has are so easy. I would take this class all over again for the experience.

Literally my favorite professor, he is a good-natured and funny guy. He made the subject more fun to learn and had conversations with the class about class policy so you know he really cares. The hw assignments are video led and although the tests can be challenging he gives you what you need to know in class.  I hope to have him for other classes.
Brent loves to gives out extra credit like a frat boy loves to ask who you know here!!!! Hes the coolest guy ever & kills it every time, definitely take his class

I loved having him as a professor. He will help you do well and clarify when needed. He tries his best to make stats interesting and does so successfully. One of the best teachers I have had at the U of A. He also makes sure the majority understands concepts and will review needed materials when he senses confusion.

Brent has been one of my favorite teachers at the U of A. He constantly offers opportunities to gain extra points on tests and is willing to move the entire classes test back if it falls on the same day as an acct 200 or econ 200 test. He drafts effective study guides for tests and one day brought his dog into a lecture for de-stressing purposes.

Brent was awesome, he made class fun and enjoyable. Not going to lie, this class was difficult but he is super helpful and if you visit any of his or the preceptor's office hours you should be good. If you are going to take stats, make sure it's with him!!

He is really a great teacher to teach the basic business statistic knowledge. He is kind and patient to answer your question. He responses your email quickly. Practice and review will give for each exam. If you review good, the test will be a piece of cake. He curves and gives extra credit to help his students get their expected grades. 100% enjoy!

This was a good enough course, the material is not my favorite but it's easy to get through with Brent. His classes are very fun and you will get free snacks from him all the time. Lectures can be easy to get lost, but he's there for clarification after and posts them online. Exam/assignment heavy, although all assignments are given a video guide.

Professor Marinan made this class fun by keeping the classes entertaining, while still teaching us so much about Business Statistics. He really cares about his students succeeding, and I enjoyed my time in his class.

Professor is engaging in large class and made the stats material interesting connecting it to real world events. The exam guides are a great refresher before the exams along with the lecture slides and videos.

I loved this professor!! This class does require you to study outside of class because his lectures are short, but he gives lots of extra credit to make up for it. He really cares about his students and will help you if you're struggling. His assignments are easy to follow with videos too, so it's hard to get lost. Highly recommend for BNAN 276.
He is the best professor at this school. SUCH A NICE GUY. He actually cares about his students. Such a good instructor with lots of EC. Very good communication and very fair on grading.
Brent is the best professor I had last year.  Was intimidated by some of the topics but made it easier to understand.  Used the Tableau he taught during my summer intern.
Literally so much EC. Don't try to cheat on take home quizzes. he knows

He is a GREAT teacher. He is so generous,if you show up, you can get extra credit! He is amazing, he gave us snacks and April bags(birthday bags), he even brought his dog as an assistant. Class was fun! He is amazing!! If you are taking BNAN 276, take with him, you won't regret!
THE GOAT! show up to class and get extra credit. easy A. awesome cool guy, would take again. So easy.
Brent is very likable, open to student suggestions for how class should work, and flexible. At first glance, the concepts can be difficult to digest but Brent does well explaining them. Good class.
I took BNAD 276 with Brent over the summer as a 7 week course. The class was fast pace but manageable as it was well organized. There is extra credit opportunities and the grading is very fair.

Fantastic professor. He does everything in his power to help students succeed. The class is organized and taught very well, lectures are clear and there are plenty of opportunities to get help and earn extra credit.

Brent was a super nice and friendly guy. You can tell he really wants his students to succeed. He lets you take the tests online and offers a lot of flexibility. He really listens to the students so if you want something changed you can change it. SO much extra credit
If you have the chance to have this professor TAKE HIM, he da best

Dr Marinan was extremely engaging all semester. Dr. Marinan also really helps you understand the software Tableau Public, which is a really useful tool in the business world. His step by step videos of the projects make it easy to understand both what you are looking for and how to navigate the software. I would definitely take this course again.

Brent is super chill. Best professor I've ever had here. He quite literally asked us at the beginning of the semester how we wanted it to go. We said, online tests, multiple choice, lots of EC, etc... he said okay and that's how it's been all semester. Highly highly recommend.

Breaks down complex topics well. He summarizes the info from the book into digestible synopsis' so we don't have to read the book, which streamlines the process. The info course content can be daunting at times, but Professor Marinan made the course interesting and straight-forward.

Brent is an amazing professor!! The first half of this class with the other professor is horrible, but his teaching was seriously the light at the end of the tunnel. His style of teaching and extra credit opportunities saved my grade. He also made the lectures infinitely more interesting to listen to.

I'm not going to lie, this may have been one of my favorite courses at the UofA so far. Brent was an AMAZING professor and honestly just seems like a genuine guy. He takes the time to ensure understanding, he's funny dude, offers tons of EC opportunities. I'm not gonna say it's an easy A, but you won't regret taking it with Brent.

Brent is awesome. He gives so many extra credit opportunities, and really wants you to succeed in the class. Projects aren't too difficult at all, and there are grace periods for lateness. Tests aren't too bad if you're confident and/or study, and there are plenty of other grades to cushion that. Super helpful and respectful guy.

Probably my favorite professor I've ever had. Super engaging and inclusive during his lectures, actually looked forward to this class. Super helpful and receptive to questions. It would be a mistake not to take this class with him!

Perhaps one of the most understanding professors I've had. His course is structured very well and his exams are not overly complicated. His lectures are always very engaging. He offers so many opportunities for extra credit, but as long as you attend lectures and take notes, you'll be fine. He's managed to make the course interactive and engaging.
Brent is amazing! He is so kind and understanding. He teaches so well but please go to class, that is where you will learn the majority of the material. He really wants you to do well.

Pre-session 3 week course - Easy to keep up with assignments and lecture videos are very helpful. All the tests are online and open note plus you get 2 hours and that is plenty of time to look up answers that you need to double check. I would highly recommend precession with Professor Marinan.
The class was pretty easy for the most part but there are many symbols you have to remember. He gives extra credit often so I highly recommend going to class. Overall a great professor!

Prof. Marinan is an extremely nice professor. He responds to emails quick as well. He gives out a significant amount of extra credit when he does random attendance checks throughout the semester. The class is heavily weighted on the tests so make sure to do very good on those and study!

Brent's class was usually fun, and he gives tons of extra credit. He makes the tests and homework extremely easy, often dropping or cancelling assignments so I wasn't forced to learn very much in the class. Aside from that he is passionate about stats and is helpful and accesible if needed

Brent is a great guy and fun professor. However, his teaching style is a bit all over the place and makes it difficult to retain the information. While he does give out a TON of extra credit, I have been in the class for 3 months and feel like I haven't learned anything.
He's amazing! an Absolute Gem!
He is an absolutely amazing, best teacher ever. LITERAL ANGEL. Very understanding and gives lots of extra credit. Recommend 100/100, he is so freaking nice i cant.

I took both BNAD 276 & 277 with Brent and got A's in both. Pay attention to the synopsis he puts out. Most questions on the quizzes/exams come from that. He gives extra credit to those that show up a lot of the time so don't skip class.

This is by far one of the best professors I have taken at UA. He is hilarious and makes the class enjoyable. Statistics is an annoying subject but he actually makes you want to be there to learn the material. He gives a lot of extra credit which is a plus because it helps you out when you need it most! Would definitely recommend this professor!
Exams were not easy, but definitely fair.  Attend the lectures and you will learn alot.  Have him next semester again and looking forward to it.
Really cool guy, terrible professor. Lectures do not reflect the tests.
Brent's class was my favorite of the semester. The class was not easy but he made it relatable to real-life applications and made me a lot more interested in statistics.
Good teacher. Easy to get an A in the class if you do his study guides.
I love Brent. He is the best professor I've had at U of A overall
I found this class to be my hardest one at the University of Arizona thus far. One good thing I can say about Brent is that he gives out a lot of extra credit.

Brent is a funny, nice professor and really cares about his students, but is an AWFUL teacher. His lectures are inconsistent, and he does not make a lot of sense explaining and working through problems. Stats is difficult as it is, but the structure of his class made it even harder to be successful. Gives a lot of extra credit.

Suzanne Delaney.review_text: People didn't like how 'she treated us like children', but regardless of that, she was at least organized, super nice, and made lectures somewhat exciting. Homework every lecture, but SUPER easy and she goes over it in class usually. Tests are easy if you do the Just-For-Fun assignments she provides. Easy pass if you just do what she asks of you.

Yeah she treats us like we're 5 and gives assigned seats and her powerpoint animations are ridiculous, but she is genuinely nice and just trying to get everyone to learn the material. If you go to her office hours she will talk to you for hours long and try to help you as much as she can. Exams are easy! DON'T SKIP THE PLOUS READINGS!
if you can skip this class do it, you will not pass...
EXAMS ARE VERY DIFFICULT.  Boring lectures and she is not the best teacher so be warned.
this class was extremely difficult. on top of that her lectures were death
tests are impossible.. class is extremely difficult. WOW

Does not prepare students to her impossible exams. The materiel on the slides is way basic comparing to what you'll get on the test. This class is useless in real life because nothing is true 100%. they claim that its better to be approximately right than totally wrong but i say if you are not 100% sure that right then you are wrong no matter what.

Delaney is hands down the worst teacher I have had in college. She makes the study guides for tests 10+ pages long for a 40 question test so it is impossible to know what you will be tested on. Have to force myself not to fall asleep in class. Ridiculous amount of homework and reading. DO NOT take this class. Take it at Pima if you can.

Worst teacher I have had at the U of A. Doesn't know how to teach the class, terrible at math and thats half of the class. Doesn't curve even though everyone is failing. Class isn't hard she is just a bad teacher.

One of the best at the University. Statistics can be confusing for many people. It feels as if she was born to teach. Makes everything so easy if you just pay attention and listen. I didn't even have to take notes thanks to her teaching style.

Delaney is the sweetest professor I have had in my three years at U of A. She explains topics maybe too in depth but tries hard to help students. Does go off on tangents, but has a lot of experience in the area. Tests are based mainly on lecture, but she offers a large amount of exam reviews! Some homework but for sure do able.

She gives homework everyday but you will begin to notice that the TA's only look for if you did the homework or not rather than if you did it correctly. She gives you the textbook though a URL to a website she made and if you just change the year in the URL you can get the answers from the year before. Listen in lecture and you will get an A
The material is not challenging, but she treats you like you're 5

Material is straight forward and she teaches it well. 4 exams, all multiple choice makes them simple. A few tricky questions here and there, but nothing to worry over. Lots of homework, but it doesn't take long to do. Textbook isn't really needed, never even opened it, she goes so thoroughly over every topic in class. Take if you can.
Tests will be tough.. if you pay attention in lecture you might get a C but this class will be very hard nonetheless.  Do your homework and make sure you understand the material so you do not fail.
when the going gets tough.. bear down or in this class just fail. good luck kids
ALL four tests are extremely difficult.  lectures are very boring. homework is boring. lectures about same material then tests are all new material it makes no sense.

This teacher is ungodly annoying. Not only does the voice of this professor get extremely irritating, this class makes me wanna absolutely die. The way she walks up and down the aisles to "check" on you with uncomfortable staring, I cannot say I've met a professor like this. Super energetic, but an annoying kind that makes the class period so long.

I don't know if it is up to the professors that teach BNAD 276 but the program they use is absolutely horrible. It's clear that she doesn't have any public speaking skills and her voice over of lectures is a pain to listen to. Do NOT take this class online, it will discourage you from learning from her. You're honestly better off reading the book.

I took 276 with Marinan but they split the class between the two of them for the first and second half of the semester. Delaney gives hard pop quizzes with no time to do them. No extra credit at all. Not that good of a teacher. If possible take with Marinan. He is a far better teacher who is a lot more precise with his speech and a better prof.
Don't take her class. Period. It's so fast. Waste of time, don't learn anything. They expect you to understand everything in 2 seconds. So many quizzes that are rushed.
Doesn't provide with good study guides, uses very heavy terms which are not even required. teaches as if each student is a pro at stats.

She's really nice but her lectures were so long and repetitive. I could've just read the lecture slides and understood everything in less than half the time but she took attendance. Easy homework due each class and tests were multiple choice and reasonable, we even practiced questions with her in class before the test date.

Suzanne Delaney is amazing. She really cares about her students and makes herself available. 75% of the final grade is based on Exams, so use her exam prep materials, which are spot on, and you will do well. She also has great lectures, the book is so hard to read, so I just used her lectures. They make complex material simple, the book sucked.

Delaney is a fantastic professor! Funny and engaging. She posts every lecture on d2l after class, unlike most professors. The homework is easy and genuinely helps you understand the material. The tests are also easy and consistently have repeat questions from previous tests and from the review problems given during lectures. TAKE THIS CLASS!!
Professor Delaney is one of my favorite teachers. She tries to keep lectures fun, and really knows her material! Very sweet lady who cares about your success.

It's only my first semester at the U of A but I can tell she's one of the best. Really funny with a passion to make concepts clear and for once, powerpoints were actually interesting. A bunch of homework, multiple pages every day but it's easy. Only knock on the class would be that there's assigned seats, but besides that I highly recommend her.
This class was extremely difficult. Lectures are so long and boring it's so hard to stay focused. A LOT of homework assignments.

lectures are really long and she does not teach well. This is pretty basic info but she just fails to teach it well and instead just laughs in class all the time. I would really appreciate if another teacher taught this class because no one learned in this one.

Delaney was an extremely hard professor. Unfortunately, she did not make an already hard subject any less confusing or easy to grasp. She seemed like a very nice person, but she just could not dumb down the material at all. Also, her exams were killer!!

Dr. Delaney is fine. She seems a little bit out of her element teaching a business stats class. Nothing in class was business related in the slightest, and I have not clue what differentiates this from a regular stats class. I phoned it in the entire time. Dr. Delaney sometimes said some comments during her lecture that rubbed me the wrong way.

Wasn't too good at teaching. She went through her slides really fast but she uploads every single one onto D2L so you could download it and go over it at your own pace. I did this after every lesson and got an A on every exams. Exams were super easy. The people who say it's hard were probably the ones not giving two craps about the class.

Professor Delaney is a psychology professor teaching business statistics. Her lectures have not had any content related to business and our based on simple statistic principles. The tests feel like they were made for elementary kids. Should be your easiest A before heading to Eller.

This class is supposed to be really easy. However, the Prof is just making things harder. She really is caring and tries her best but she just goes too fast and to be honest she is not really good at getting to the point. Everything will seem very blurry in class but her homeworks really help for studying. Gives second chances. Easy tests.

Prof Delaney shouldn't be teaching this class. So hard to understand her recorded lectures since she is not a good public speaker, very hard to listen to her while doing the lectures. Contrary to Fall/Spring term, the summer term (5-week specifically) it is closed note and all 3 exams are 75% of your grade.

If you are taking BNAD 276 take Brent Marinan's class. His class has way more extra credit opportunities, fewer tests, way less homework, and you take your tests online. If you take BNAD 276 through this professor be prepared to do way more work than your peers will.
Didn't engage with students.

This class is very lecture heavy and sometimes a little confusing but Delaney makes the entire class seem easier. She has a lot of assignments both in class and out but they're always a very flexible due date and pretty easy, just for a better understanding.
lots of homework and long lectures but she is a nice professor and teaches well

I really enjoyed her BNAD 276 course. Her lectures are very easy to follow and you will learn a lot in class. Some homework assignments can be a little tedious but they are helpful. If you just do what you're supposed to, you should get an A with no problem

Makes a boring subject very easy to follow and understand if you follow her lectures. Very engaging teacher and one of the professors who makes a big lecture class a lot more bearable. Clicker attendance every class, with a little extra credit offered near the end of the semester. Hand written homework assignments fairly often that is helpful.
Such a great teacher. Very knowledgeable and did not use the text books much except for a homework grade at the end of the semester.

Statistics is a hard/boring class but Dr. Delaney makes it as painless as possible. She has funny animations on her slides and tries her best to make sure that her students understand before moving on to other material. If you have to take stats , take it with Dr. Delaney.

Her lectures are so boring but the overall class isn't too bad. Tests are worth 20% each so definitely study! She does clicker attendance every class and also does in class writing assignments often. If you study you'll do just fine. She gives you the answers to almost all homework and accepts late work the entire semester.

You definitely get back what you put into it...  but the extra effort is SO worth it.  Delaney is a STATS rock star.  I am so glad I dropped my course at Pima and opted for this one.  Excellent instruction, and one of the few courses where you feel you got MUCH MORE than you paid for!

Delaney is a great teacher, sometimes her powerpoints can be a little unorganized but overall she tries to make each lecture as painless as possible. There is homework for pretty much each class period but its not hard just tedious. Overall the first test is the hardest. There are assigned seats in this class which is weird but she's really nice!

Stats itself is a difficult concept to grasp but Delaney is amazing at teaching it. The content is tough but reviewing the lecture slides and reading the textbook on your own time will do wonders. On top of that she is more than willing to help during her office hours. An A is very much attainable.

Delaney's class was hands down my favorite class this semester. Lectures can be a bit on the boring side, but I guarantee that if you pay attention, do the assignments, and review the powerpoint lectures before each exam, you will receive an A. Exams are cumulative and the lessons go over past material so that the knowledge can be intuitive.

she takes back her word many times and doesn't follow through which in the end hurts your grades as a student. she is not clear and repeats the same information over and over but in different ways to make it sound harder than it is. not a good teacher at all. should be fired. not even a business class. seems more like a psych class. she's not good.

This class was kind of weird. First two exams were hard. Even after studying notes, powerpoints, and the book, there would be questions that I had no idea what the answer was. Last two exams were a piece of cake. You'll end up with an A if you study and put in a decent amount of effort. Don't let the first two exams scare you off.
So boring that you will never want to go to class and never pay attention. Homework is graded easily, tests need a few days to study for and are cumulative.

This class is not as easy as everyone makes it out to be if you've never seen stats before. Topics are challenging. Tests 1 and 2 are hard, 3 and 4 easy. DO NOT BUY THE BOOK- YOU WILL NOT USE IT. Save yourself the 180 bucks

This class is absolutely terrible! I felt like I was being brainwashed every time sitting through her class. I did so many activities to keep myself entertained. You have to take this if your is business, but honestly I feel bad for the rest of you.

Speaks like we are 5, and always so happy. Makes you go to class by having daily writing assigments and homework. 4 Tests fro 100 points each. First test is pretty hard. Clikcer questions and assigned seats, which blows. She's very helpful ando are the TAs. Only way to take notes is on a notebook, no laptops. Good class but boringgggg.
ONE OF THE BEST PROFESSOR

Delaney is a great teacher who cares about her students. However, this is a really boring class. Attendance is mandatory and you have assigned seats but all of the power points are online. There is homework every class but she usually goes over it before you turn it in. Tests arent that bad but they are cumulative. There are four tests and no final

Really enthusiastic teacher not teaching an exciting subject.  She does her best to keep things interesting, and is a really great teacher.  Doing the homework will help you understand the material.  Not too tough of a class if you pay attention and do the homework.  Awesome teacher!

This class may seem tough at first but very little math is involved and when it is she gives you the formulas, attendance is mandatory and hw is easy points, if you do it and turn it in you get full points regardless. Exams 1 & 2 were fairly diffucult but you can easily bring your grade to an A or B with exams 3 & 4 as they are much easier!

The teacher is so sweet and explains everything nicely. None of the subject was too difficult. There is homework due every class, but she grades it based on completeness not if its correct. You MUST read the plous readings because she has questions in the exams about them. The only bad thing is that She takes attendance and you have assigned seats.
Delaney was an enthusiastic professor who cared about her students' success. Highly Recommend

Great teacher who really wants to help her students do well. She gives you all of the homework answers and the material is stuff that should seem pretty obvious. HW is good for learning as well as grade and if you do it all she bumps your grade up. Great experience overall

Does a fantastic job of making a potentially boring subject interesting and easy to understand. Do the homework, show up to class, and you will do well. Tests are straightforward. Just cause math isn't your strong suit doesn't mean you can't do well in this course.
Terrible professor. Very hard to stay focused and doesnt explain information clearly

Great instructor. Her powerpoints and lectures are incredibly clear. The homework is inventive and allows creativity. Show up everyday so you don't miss your writing assignments which are usually free points.
Easy class, entertaining teacher
She is a great teacher... but the class is PRETTY boring. I'll admit I slipped into a deep sleep every now and then. BUT she is the nicest lady EVER, and really cares about her students.

Nicest teacher ever. She is so cute, but unfortunately the material is so so so boring, but she tries really hard to make it fun. Attendance points in class add up. Class out of fewer points so they all make a difference in the end. Tests are not hard if you put time into studying (if you dont pay attention in class).

She is a really nice teacher and wants her students to understand the concepts....the tests aren't hard at all, especially if you just put in a little extra effort studying.  Typically veryone is focusing on Blanchard class the same semester...so her class, which should be an easy A, appears harder to students than it really is.

She's really enthusiastic about teaching. Very nice and helpful. Lectures are boring. She makes you use your clicker in class. Material is straightforward, but tests are harder than you would expect. No final and barely any extra credit.
she's enthusiastic and mostly entertaining. class is a joke: 4 incredibly easy midterms and no final. attendance is monitored, but its only a small sliver of your final grade.
Awesome class, Delaney is extremely nice and helpful!  The material is straight forward and the tests are easy if you study a decent amount!

Really nice lady, boring class, fairly easy material. The tests are all multiple choice and she gives you any formulas needed. However, the tests can be more difficult than expected. She is more about teaching concepts and definitions than she calculations. Lots of homework, and little extra credit.
She is nice and tries to make lectures interesting but in reality they are very boring. Its an easy class but there is a lot of hw.

Very nice lady, always available for help during office hours. The class it self is very boring but she's really nice about helping you out and lets you turn in homework up until the final. Dont buy the text book, just borrow it from someone to do the homework. Study the slides and your good to go.

Straight forward teaching style. Great study guides help for tests and to learn what she wants you to know. Tests aren't tricky, but aren't too easy either. I thought it was a good class. It was in the Bio-East Lecture Hall and the desks were too small and seats too uncomfortable though, so be forewarned if you are in that room.

Ok, her classes are boring and she is very unstructured, but all of her slides are online, and the HWs are pretty much free points along with the Writing Assignments and she even offers exta credit. Can be tought to sit through.

Dr. Delaney is a great teacher who wants you to understand the material you are learning.  She is extremely helpful in her office hours. The test are not very tricky if you go to class and do all of the homework.
She's a pretty good teacher but there is so so so so so so so much homework.  i spend more time doing her homework than attending her class.  this is a problem.
I feel like I am in kindergarten when I walk into this class. It is pretty awesome. The class is really easy...almost a joke. About the same level or even easier than high school classes.

Very sweet teacher lady. Her first semester teaching 276 i believe and she did okay. Only thing was she didn't put our grades online ALL semester and will not respond to emails so you have no clue what your grade is. Also her study guides are useless. There is way too much information on them to study effectively. Overall, the class was simply ok.

She is by far the worst teacher I have had.  This class was supposed to be a business statistics class, but she taught it as if it were a psych stats class.  I did less than half the HW and in-class assignments, got A's on all the tests and got a B.  This class is a joke and she is a joke of a teacher as far as MGMT 276 goes!!!

Good teacher, boring topic.  Information was presented logically and step by step.  Go to class and the course is a breeze.  From what I've heard about the old 276 teachers Delaney is 1000% percent better

THE WORST TEACHER EVER. SHE CANNOT TEACH TO SAVE HER LIFE. SHE CRACKS USELESS JOKES AND WASTES OUR TIME. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS WITH HER. HER TESTS ARE HORRIBLE. SHES JUST PATHETIC AND NEEDS TO STOP TEACHER. PEOPLE KEEP SAYING SHES GREAT AT PSYCH AND SHE MIGHT BE BUT SHE DEFINITELY SHOULDN'T TEACH MGMT.

This is by far the most boring, un-engaging class I have taken at the university.  The exams are a mess and poorly worded.  She often has trouble explaining concepts because she gets them confused in her own head!  Do yourself a favor and take someone else for this class.
great teacher

Wow.  Words cannot begin to describe how useless she is as a MGMT 276 teacher.  Why would they have this psychology teacher lecture statistics? The Texts book has little to nothing to do with the exams or material taught in class.  She choose to include another text ?The Psychology of Judgment and Decision Making? which is simply a book about vari

Awesome teacher!  Glad we got her from Psych.  She is very personable and interacts comfortably with class. Always provides sufficient examples and plenty of review of the concepts.  Truly one of the best EDUCATORS at the U!
She is a really good professor. She makes learning really fun and easy. She is also pretty funny. Exams were also open notes so the class was supper easy.

She was a great teacher. She is more concept-based and doesn't make you learn how to do the math for everything which makes focusing on the actual material much easier. She is such a sweet lady, I can't emphasize enough. Also extremely understanding of any problems or struggles you have with the material. I recommend strongly!!!

Great teacher. Her lectures can be boring and you're in trouble if you miss more than a few but she will help you all day in office hours if you need it. She teaches a difficult subject but makes things easier to understand. I recommend her to anyone who asks!

Delaney has a unique ability to keep students interested in the material. Content is super boring but relevant and applicable in any real-life setting. The first two tests were SLIGHTLY tougher than the last two but honestly I studied two days before and still got As. Go to class for attendance points and do the homework and you'll be golden :)
Dr. Delaney is a terrific teacher. If you go to class, take notes, and pay attention you won't have problems. Highly recommended for Mgmt 276.

Requires 110% effort. If you don't get it you won't get it unless you spend the time to learn it and it will most definitely be outside of lectures. Lectures and slides are confusing and are not great materials to review from.

She treated the class like we were five, her slides had animations and music on them, but overall not a bad teacher. You learn the material through her lectures as well as the homework. Go to the review thats offered. That's pretty  much all I studied. I got an A without buying the book, but do the Plous reading and go to class for points.

the class can be difficult if you do not study. but the info is actuall pretty clear cut. before the tests go through the homework and slides more than just one time youll do fine. first test didn't study got an F, studied the other ones got As. do the homework and jut go to class even though lectures suck.

She's super nice, very willing to re-explain concepts, and very practical. She always makes sure you know WHY you're learning what you are. The class is super easy, don't expect math. It's sort of how statistics works, not why it works, very general. If you are an Econ major, take ECON339 instead because while she is great, you'll be unprepared.

Overall, a pretty dry class; however, Dr. Delaney is a very knowledgeable and caring professor.  The tests are challenging but put some time and effort into them and you will do just fine. Don't buy the textbook, everything is online. The last two tests were easier than the first two

Enthusiastic, helpful, and fun teacher! Boring subject but she makes it interesting. Her assignments are easy and tests are easy if you make sure to understand everything in her study guides. Assigned seating sucks but it does help to improve focus and attendance. Don't buy the book. Anyone can get an A in this class!
Attendance is extra credit. She's actually really funny and will always let you out of class early. She's sweet too.

There is a lot of homework and in class assignments but you could literally write anything on them and still get credit for them. But for your overall grade they dont even count for much. Her lectures aren't very helpful because she spends more time talking about the slide animations than the actual material. Very nice lady though lol

Annoyingly strict on attendance and in class participation(15% of grade). This wouldn't be a problem if she was a better and more engaging teacher. It was easy to miss class and earn A's on the exams. Offers little to no extra credit.

Got a 79%, only because I lost my homework and the tests are based on that. She didn't post any keys for the HW, and about 20% of every test is from HW's that she doesn't have answers for. Lectures are boring af. MY ONLY C AT THE UNIVERSITY!

all_reviews.review_text: Brent is an excellent teacher and gives quite a few extra credit opportunities. He also makes it readily apparent what will be on the test, so there is really no excuse to not succeed in this course.
Awesome professor. Favorite this semester. Class goes by quickly as always working on real world data assignments in groups. Replied so quickly to emails.

This professor was good and let us make the syllabus at the beginning of the semester, but he wasn't that great at teaching. I've never taken a stats class before, and he just read off the slides the entire time, and sometimes his slides were outdated and confusing. He did provide lots of extra credit, so he makes the class easy to pass.

Overall really nice and very understanding guy, but taking this at 8am was brutal. The lecture presentations are pretty vague and he doesn't give much detail, which leaves you to figure out how to do it on weekly group assignments. Honestly, take this for extra credit but you're better off going with any other BNAN 276 teacher.

Professor was super supportive. Many of the assignments were working together and interesting. Stats isn't the easiest but he made an early morning class much easier to get up for. Take him if you can.

Great Professor to take for BNAN 276. Would recommend him to everyone to take him. He offers extra credit left and right as long as you go in person. Great lectures, uses real-world examples. Even though I do not really enjoy business stats at all, he made it possible and easy to pass this class.
Brent was such a great professor! BNAD 276 was so easy with him and gives you many opportunities to help your grade.

Took class as a senior. Brent has been one of my favorite professors at U of A. I don't miss these large class sizes though. Took AP stats in HS and this class was at a much higher level. I don't have to take the next stat course, but hands down would take him again. Keep up with the work.

If you have to take a BNAD class Brent's the guy to do it with. He'll do anything to help his students learn & he's also super funny which makes a hard class a lil better. This class is challenging, but as long as you do ALL the practice problems he posts and go over the HW questions before a test it's manageable. He also gives some extra credit

Overall, the material was not hard, but what got me was the exams.  Majority of the class would get pretty good grades on the exams, but I didn't do well since I get nervous on them and definitely should have been more prepared.  500 Total Points: 400 points on exams (4 exams) 100 points on other things (homework/case studies/ attendance)
Had Brent for a stats class and a programming class. Explain concepts with realistic examples. Really cares about the students.

Best professor I could ever ask for tbh, tons of extra credits PLUS snacks on review days before the exam. Whoever rate this man below a 4 is def never in class because the exams and Has are so easy. I would take this class all over again for the experience.

Literally my favorite professor, he is a good-natured and funny guy. He made the subject more fun to learn and had conversations with the class about class policy so you know he really cares. The hw assignments are video led and although the tests can be challenging he gives you what you need to know in class.  I hope to have him for other classes.
Brent loves to gives out extra credit like a frat boy loves to ask who you know here!!!! Hes the coolest guy ever & kills it every time, definitely take his class

I loved having him as a professor. He will help you do well and clarify when needed. He tries his best to make stats interesting and does so successfully. One of the best teachers I have had at the U of A. He also makes sure the majority understands concepts and will review needed materials when he senses confusion.

Brent has been one of my favorite teachers at the U of A. He constantly offers opportunities to gain extra points on tests and is willing to move the entire classes test back if it falls on the same day as an acct 200 or econ 200 test. He drafts effective study guides for tests and one day brought his dog into a lecture for de-stressing purposes.

Brent was awesome, he made class fun and enjoyable. Not going to lie, this class was difficult but he is super helpful and if you visit any of his or the preceptor's office hours you should be good. If you are going to take stats, make sure it's with him!!

He is really a great teacher to teach the basic business statistic knowledge. He is kind and patient to answer your question. He responses your email quickly. Practice and review will give for each exam. If you review good, the test will be a piece of cake. He curves and gives extra credit to help his students get their expected grades. 100% enjoy!

This was a good enough course, the material is not my favorite but it's easy to get through with Brent. His classes are very fun and you will get free snacks from him all the time. Lectures can be easy to get lost, but he's there for clarification after and posts them online. Exam/assignment heavy, although all assignments are given a video guide.

Professor Marinan made this class fun by keeping the classes entertaining, while still teaching us so much about Business Statistics. He really cares about his students succeeding, and I enjoyed my time in his class.

Professor is engaging in large class and made the stats material interesting connecting it to real world events. The exam guides are a great refresher before the exams along with the lecture slides and videos.

I loved this professor!! This class does require you to study outside of class because his lectures are short, but he gives lots of extra credit to make up for it. He really cares about his students and will help you if you're struggling. His assignments are easy to follow with videos too, so it's hard to get lost. Highly recommend for BNAN 276.
He is the best professor at this school. SUCH A NICE GUY. He actually cares about his students. Such a good instructor with lots of EC. Very good communication and very fair on grading.
Brent is the best professor I had last year.  Was intimidated by some of the topics but made it easier to understand.  Used the Tableau he taught during my summer intern.
Literally so much EC. Don't try to cheat on take home quizzes. he knows

He is a GREAT teacher. He is so generous,if you show up, you can get extra credit! He is amazing, he gave us snacks and April bags(birthday bags), he even brought his dog as an assistant. Class was fun! He is amazing!! If you are taking BNAN 276, take with him, you won't regret!
THE GOAT! show up to class and get extra credit. easy A. awesome cool guy, would take again. So easy.
Brent is very likable, open to student suggestions for how class should work, and flexible. At first glance, the concepts can be difficult to digest but Brent does well explaining them. Good class.
I took BNAD 276 with Brent over the summer as a 7 week course. The class was fast pace but manageable as it was well organized. There is extra credit opportunities and the grading is very fair.

Fantastic professor. He does everything in his power to help students succeed. The class is organized and taught very well, lectures are clear and there are plenty of opportunities to get help and earn extra credit.

Brent was a super nice and friendly guy. You can tell he really wants his students to succeed. He lets you take the tests online and offers a lot of flexibility. He really listens to the students so if you want something changed you can change it. SO much extra credit
If you have the chance to have this professor TAKE HIM, he da best

Dr Marinan was extremely engaging all semester. Dr. Marinan also really helps you understand the software Tableau Public, which is a really useful tool in the business world. His step by step videos of the projects make it easy to understand both what you are looking for and how to navigate the software. I would definitely take this course again.

Brent is super chill. Best professor I've ever had here. He quite literally asked us at the beginning of the semester how we wanted it to go. We said, online tests, multiple choice, lots of EC, etc... he said okay and that's how it's been all semester. Highly highly recommend.

Breaks down complex topics well. He summarizes the info from the book into digestible synopsis' so we don't have to read the book, which streamlines the process. The info course content can be daunting at times, but Professor Marinan made the course interesting and straight-forward.

Brent is an amazing professor!! The first half of this class with the other professor is horrible, but his teaching was seriously the light at the end of the tunnel. His style of teaching and extra credit opportunities saved my grade. He also made the lectures infinitely more interesting to listen to.

I'm not going to lie, this may have been one of my favorite courses at the UofA so far. Brent was an AMAZING professor and honestly just seems like a genuine guy. He takes the time to ensure understanding, he's funny dude, offers tons of EC opportunities. I'm not gonna say it's an easy A, but you won't regret taking it with Brent.

Brent is awesome. He gives so many extra credit opportunities, and really wants you to succeed in the class. Projects aren't too difficult at all, and there are grace periods for lateness. Tests aren't too bad if you're confident and/or study, and there are plenty of other grades to cushion that. Super helpful and respectful guy.

Probably my favorite professor I've ever had. Super engaging and inclusive during his lectures, actually looked forward to this class. Super helpful and receptive to questions. It would be a mistake not to take this class with him!

Perhaps one of the most understanding professors I've had. His course is structured very well and his exams are not overly complicated. His lectures are always very engaging. He offers so many opportunities for extra credit, but as long as you attend lectures and take notes, you'll be fine. He's managed to make the course interactive and engaging.
Brent is amazing! He is so kind and understanding. He teaches so well but please go to class, that is where you will learn the majority of the material. He really wants you to do well.

Pre-session 3 week course - Easy to keep up with assignments and lecture videos are very helpful. All the tests are online and open note plus you get 2 hours and that is plenty of time to look up answers that you need to double check. I would highly recommend precession with Professor Marinan.
The class was pretty easy for the most part but there are many symbols you have to remember. He gives extra credit often so I highly recommend going to class. Overall a great professor!

Prof. Marinan is an extremely nice professor. He responds to emails quick as well. He gives out a significant amount of extra credit when he does random attendance checks throughout the semester. The class is heavily weighted on the tests so make sure to do very good on those and study!

Brent's class was usually fun, and he gives tons of extra credit. He makes the tests and homework extremely easy, often dropping or cancelling assignments so I wasn't forced to learn very much in the class. Aside from that he is passionate about stats and is helpful and accesible if needed

Brent is a great guy and fun professor. However, his teaching style is a bit all over the place and makes it difficult to retain the information. While he does give out a TON of extra credit, I have been in the class for 3 months and feel like I haven't learned anything.
He's amazing! an Absolute Gem!
He is an absolutely amazing, best teacher ever. LITERAL ANGEL. Very understanding and gives lots of extra credit. Recommend 100/100, he is so freaking nice i cant.

I took both BNAD 276 & 277 with Brent and got A's in both. Pay attention to the synopsis he puts out. Most questions on the quizzes/exams come from that. He gives extra credit to those that show up a lot of the time so don't skip class.

This is by far one of the best professors I have taken at UA. He is hilarious and makes the class enjoyable. Statistics is an annoying subject but he actually makes you want to be there to learn the material. He gives a lot of extra credit which is a plus because it helps you out when you need it most! Would definitely recommend this professor!
Exams were not easy, but definitely fair.  Attend the lectures and you will learn alot.  Have him next semester again and looking forward to it.
Really cool guy, terrible professor. Lectures do not reflect the tests.
Brent's class was my favorite of the semester. The class was not easy but he made it relatable to real-life applications and made me a lot more interested in statistics.
Good teacher. Easy to get an A in the class if you do his study guides.
I love Brent. He is the best professor I've had at U of A overall
I found this class to be my hardest one at the University of Arizona thus far. One good thing I can say about Brent is that he gives out a lot of extra credit.

Brent is a funny, nice professor and really cares about his students, but is an AWFUL teacher. His lectures are inconsistent, and he does not make a lot of sense explaining and working through problems. Stats is difficult as it is, but the structure of his class made it even harder to be successful. Gives a lot of extra credit.

People didn't like how 'she treated us like children', but regardless of that, she was at least organized, super nice, and made lectures somewhat exciting. Homework every lecture, but SUPER easy and she goes over it in class usually. Tests are easy if you do the Just-For-Fun assignments she provides. Easy pass if you just do what she asks of you.

Yeah she treats us like we're 5 and gives assigned seats and her powerpoint animations are ridiculous, but she is genuinely nice and just trying to get everyone to learn the material. If you go to her office hours she will talk to you for hours long and try to help you as much as she can. Exams are easy! DON'T SKIP THE PLOUS READINGS!
if you can skip this class do it, you will not pass...
EXAMS ARE VERY DIFFICULT.  Boring lectures and she is not the best teacher so be warned.
this class was extremely difficult. on top of that her lectures were death
tests are impossible.. class is extremely difficult. WOW

Does not prepare students to her impossible exams. The materiel on the slides is way basic comparing to what you'll get on the test. This class is useless in real life because nothing is true 100%. they claim that its better to be approximately right than totally wrong but i say if you are not 100% sure that right then you are wrong no matter what.

Delaney is hands down the worst teacher I have had in college. She makes the study guides for tests 10+ pages long for a 40 question test so it is impossible to know what you will be tested on. Have to force myself not to fall asleep in class. Ridiculous amount of homework and reading. DO NOT take this class. Take it at Pima if you can.

Worst teacher I have had at the U of A. Doesn't know how to teach the class, terrible at math and thats half of the class. Doesn't curve even though everyone is failing. Class isn't hard she is just a bad teacher.

One of the best at the University. Statistics can be confusing for many people. It feels as if she was born to teach. Makes everything so easy if you just pay attention and listen. I didn't even have to take notes thanks to her teaching style.

Delaney is the sweetest professor I have had in my three years at U of A. She explains topics maybe too in depth but tries hard to help students. Does go off on tangents, but has a lot of experience in the area. Tests are based mainly on lecture, but she offers a large amount of exam reviews! Some homework but for sure do able.

She gives homework everyday but you will begin to notice that the TA's only look for if you did the homework or not rather than if you did it correctly. She gives you the textbook though a URL to a website she made and if you just change the year in the URL you can get the answers from the year before. Listen in lecture and you will get an A
The material is not challenging, but she treats you like you're 5

Material is straight forward and she teaches it well. 4 exams, all multiple choice makes them simple. A few tricky questions here and there, but nothing to worry over. Lots of homework, but it doesn't take long to do. Textbook isn't really needed, never even opened it, she goes so thoroughly over every topic in class. Take if you can.
Tests will be tough.. if you pay attention in lecture you might get a C but this class will be very hard nonetheless.  Do your homework and make sure you understand the material so you do not fail.
when the going gets tough.. bear down or in this class just fail. good luck kids
ALL four tests are extremely difficult.  lectures are very boring. homework is boring. lectures about same material then tests are all new material it makes no sense.

This teacher is ungodly annoying. Not only does the voice of this professor get extremely irritating, this class makes me wanna absolutely die. The way she walks up and down the aisles to "check" on you with uncomfortable staring, I cannot say I've met a professor like this. Super energetic, but an annoying kind that makes the class period so long.

I don't know if it is up to the professors that teach BNAD 276 but the program they use is absolutely horrible. It's clear that she doesn't have any public speaking skills and her voice over of lectures is a pain to listen to. Do NOT take this class online, it will discourage you from learning from her. You're honestly better off reading the book.

I took 276 with Marinan but they split the class between the two of them for the first and second half of the semester. Delaney gives hard pop quizzes with no time to do them. No extra credit at all. Not that good of a teacher. If possible take with Marinan. He is a far better teacher who is a lot more precise with his speech and a better prof.
Don't take her class. Period. It's so fast. Waste of time, don't learn anything. They expect you to understand everything in 2 seconds. So many quizzes that are rushed.
Doesn't provide with good study guides, uses very heavy terms which are not even required. teaches as if each student is a pro at stats.

She's really nice but her lectures were so long and repetitive. I could've just read the lecture slides and understood everything in less than half the time but she took attendance. Easy homework due each class and tests were multiple choice and reasonable, we even practiced questions with her in class before the test date.

Suzanne Delaney is amazing. She really cares about her students and makes herself available. 75% of the final grade is based on Exams, so use her exam prep materials, which are spot on, and you will do well. She also has great lectures, the book is so hard to read, so I just used her lectures. They make complex material simple, the book sucked.

Delaney is a fantastic professor! Funny and engaging. She posts every lecture on d2l after class, unlike most professors. The homework is easy and genuinely helps you understand the material. The tests are also easy and consistently have repeat questions from previous tests and from the review problems given during lectures. TAKE THIS CLASS!!
Professor Delaney is one of my favorite teachers. She tries to keep lectures fun, and really knows her material! Very sweet lady who cares about your success.

It's only my first semester at the U of A but I can tell she's one of the best. Really funny with a passion to make concepts clear and for once, powerpoints were actually interesting. A bunch of homework, multiple pages every day but it's easy. Only knock on the class would be that there's assigned seats, but besides that I highly recommend her.
This class was extremely difficult. Lectures are so long and boring it's so hard to stay focused. A LOT of homework assignments.

lectures are really long and she does not teach well. This is pretty basic info but she just fails to teach it well and instead just laughs in class all the time. I would really appreciate if another teacher taught this class because no one learned in this one.

Delaney was an extremely hard professor. Unfortunately, she did not make an already hard subject any less confusing or easy to grasp. She seemed like a very nice person, but she just could not dumb down the material at all. Also, her exams were killer!!

Dr. Delaney is fine. She seems a little bit out of her element teaching a business stats class. Nothing in class was business related in the slightest, and I have not clue what differentiates this from a regular stats class. I phoned it in the entire time. Dr. Delaney sometimes said some comments during her lecture that rubbed me the wrong way.

Wasn't too good at teaching. She went through her slides really fast but she uploads every single one onto D2L so you could download it and go over it at your own pace. I did this after every lesson and got an A on every exams. Exams were super easy. The people who say it's hard were probably the ones not giving two craps about the class.

Professor Delaney is a psychology professor teaching business statistics. Her lectures have not had any content related to business and our based on simple statistic principles. The tests feel like they were made for elementary kids. Should be your easiest A before heading to Eller.

This class is supposed to be really easy. However, the Prof is just making things harder. She really is caring and tries her best but she just goes too fast and to be honest she is not really good at getting to the point. Everything will seem very blurry in class but her homeworks really help for studying. Gives second chances. Easy tests.

Prof Delaney shouldn't be teaching this class. So hard to understand her recorded lectures since she is not a good public speaker, very hard to listen to her while doing the lectures. Contrary to Fall/Spring term, the summer term (5-week specifically) it is closed note and all 3 exams are 75% of your grade.

If you are taking BNAD 276 take Brent Marinan's class. His class has way more extra credit opportunities, fewer tests, way less homework, and you take your tests online. If you take BNAD 276 through this professor be prepared to do way more work than your peers will.
Didn't engage with students.

This class is very lecture heavy and sometimes a little confusing but Delaney makes the entire class seem easier. She has a lot of assignments both in class and out but they're always a very flexible due date and pretty easy, just for a better understanding.
lots of homework and long lectures but she is a nice professor and teaches well

I really enjoyed her BNAD 276 course. Her lectures are very easy to follow and you will learn a lot in class. Some homework assignments can be a little tedious but they are helpful. If you just do what you're supposed to, you should get an A with no problem

Makes a boring subject very easy to follow and understand if you follow her lectures. Very engaging teacher and one of the professors who makes a big lecture class a lot more bearable. Clicker attendance every class, with a little extra credit offered near the end of the semester. Hand written homework assignments fairly often that is helpful.
Such a great teacher. Very knowledgeable and did not use the text books much except for a homework grade at the end of the semester.

Statistics is a hard/boring class but Dr. Delaney makes it as painless as possible. She has funny animations on her slides and tries her best to make sure that her students understand before moving on to other material. If you have to take stats , take it with Dr. Delaney.

Her lectures are so boring but the overall class isn't too bad. Tests are worth 20% each so definitely study! She does clicker attendance every class and also does in class writing assignments often. If you study you'll do just fine. She gives you the answers to almost all homework and accepts late work the entire semester.

You definitely get back what you put into it...  but the extra effort is SO worth it.  Delaney is a STATS rock star.  I am so glad I dropped my course at Pima and opted for this one.  Excellent instruction, and one of the few courses where you feel you got MUCH MORE than you paid for!

Delaney is a great teacher, sometimes her powerpoints can be a little unorganized but overall she tries to make each lecture as painless as possible. There is homework for pretty much each class period but its not hard just tedious. Overall the first test is the hardest. There are assigned seats in this class which is weird but she's really nice!

Stats itself is a difficult concept to grasp but Delaney is amazing at teaching it. The content is tough but reviewing the lecture slides and reading the textbook on your own time will do wonders. On top of that she is more than willing to help during her office hours. An A is very much attainable.

Delaney's class was hands down my favorite class this semester. Lectures can be a bit on the boring side, but I guarantee that if you pay attention, do the assignments, and review the powerpoint lectures before each exam, you will receive an A. Exams are cumulative and the lessons go over past material so that the knowledge can be intuitive.

she takes back her word many times and doesn't follow through which in the end hurts your grades as a student. she is not clear and repeats the same information over and over but in different ways to make it sound harder than it is. not a good teacher at all. should be fired. not even a business class. seems more like a psych class. she's not good.

This class was kind of weird. First two exams were hard. Even after studying notes, powerpoints, and the book, there would be questions that I had no idea what the answer was. Last two exams were a piece of cake. You'll end up with an A if you study and put in a decent amount of effort. Don't let the first two exams scare you off.
So boring that you will never want to go to class and never pay attention. Homework is graded easily, tests need a few days to study for and are cumulative.

This class is not as easy as everyone makes it out to be if you've never seen stats before. Topics are challenging. Tests 1 and 2 are hard, 3 and 4 easy. DO NOT BUY THE BOOK- YOU WILL NOT USE IT. Save yourself the 180 bucks

This class is absolutely terrible! I felt like I was being brainwashed every time sitting through her class. I did so many activities to keep myself entertained. You have to take this if your is business, but honestly I feel bad for the rest of you.

Speaks like we are 5, and always so happy. Makes you go to class by having daily writing assigments and homework. 4 Tests fro 100 points each. First test is pretty hard. Clikcer questions and assigned seats, which blows. She's very helpful ando are the TAs. Only way to take notes is on a notebook, no laptops. Good class but boringgggg.
ONE OF THE BEST PROFESSOR

Delaney is a great teacher who cares about her students. However, this is a really boring class. Attendance is mandatory and you have assigned seats but all of the power points are online. There is homework every class but she usually goes over it before you turn it in. Tests arent that bad but they are cumulative. There are four tests and no final

Really enthusiastic teacher not teaching an exciting subject.  She does her best to keep things interesting, and is a really great teacher.  Doing the homework will help you understand the material.  Not too tough of a class if you pay attention and do the homework.  Awesome teacher!

This class may seem tough at first but very little math is involved and when it is she gives you the formulas, attendance is mandatory and hw is easy points, if you do it and turn it in you get full points regardless. Exams 1 & 2 were fairly diffucult but you can easily bring your grade to an A or B with exams 3 & 4 as they are much easier!

The teacher is so sweet and explains everything nicely. None of the subject was too difficult. There is homework due every class, but she grades it based on completeness not if its correct. You MUST read the plous readings because she has questions in the exams about them. The only bad thing is that She takes attendance and you have assigned seats.
Delaney was an enthusiastic professor who cared about her students' success. Highly Recommend

Great teacher who really wants to help her students do well. She gives you all of the homework answers and the material is stuff that should seem pretty obvious. HW is good for learning as well as grade and if you do it all she bumps your grade up. Great experience overall

Does a fantastic job of making a potentially boring subject interesting and easy to understand. Do the homework, show up to class, and you will do well. Tests are straightforward. Just cause math isn't your strong suit doesn't mean you can't do well in this course.
Terrible professor. Very hard to stay focused and doesnt explain information clearly

Great instructor. Her powerpoints and lectures are incredibly clear. The homework is inventive and allows creativity. Show up everyday so you don't miss your writing assignments which are usually free points.
Easy class, entertaining teacher
She is a great teacher... but the class is PRETTY boring. I'll admit I slipped into a deep sleep every now and then. BUT she is the nicest lady EVER, and really cares about her students.

Nicest teacher ever. She is so cute, but unfortunately the material is so so so boring, but she tries really hard to make it fun. Attendance points in class add up. Class out of fewer points so they all make a difference in the end. Tests are not hard if you put time into studying (if you dont pay attention in class).

She is a really nice teacher and wants her students to understand the concepts....the tests aren't hard at all, especially if you just put in a little extra effort studying.  Typically veryone is focusing on Blanchard class the same semester...so her class, which should be an easy A, appears harder to students than it really is.

She's really enthusiastic about teaching. Very nice and helpful. Lectures are boring. She makes you use your clicker in class. Material is straightforward, but tests are harder than you would expect. No final and barely any extra credit.
she's enthusiastic and mostly entertaining. class is a joke: 4 incredibly easy midterms and no final. attendance is monitored, but its only a small sliver of your final grade.
Awesome class, Delaney is extremely nice and helpful!  The material is straight forward and the tests are easy if you study a decent amount!

Really nice lady, boring class, fairly easy material. The tests are all multiple choice and she gives you any formulas needed. However, the tests can be more difficult than expected. She is more about teaching concepts and definitions than she calculations. Lots of homework, and little extra credit.
She is nice and tries to make lectures interesting but in reality they are very boring. Its an easy class but there is a lot of hw.

Very nice lady, always available for help during office hours. The class it self is very boring but she's really nice about helping you out and lets you turn in homework up until the final. Dont buy the text book, just borrow it from someone to do the homework. Study the slides and your good to go.

Straight forward teaching style. Great study guides help for tests and to learn what she wants you to know. Tests aren't tricky, but aren't too easy either. I thought it was a good class. It was in the Bio-East Lecture Hall and the desks were too small and seats too uncomfortable though, so be forewarned if you are in that room.

Ok, her classes are boring and she is very unstructured, but all of her slides are online, and the HWs are pretty much free points along with the Writing Assignments and she even offers exta credit. Can be tought to sit through.

Dr. Delaney is a great teacher who wants you to understand the material you are learning.  She is extremely helpful in her office hours. The test are not very tricky if you go to class and do all of the homework.
She's a pretty good teacher but there is so so so so so so so much homework.  i spend more time doing her homework than attending her class.  this is a problem.
I feel like I am in kindergarten when I walk into this class. It is pretty awesome. The class is really easy...almost a joke. About the same level or even easier than high school classes.

Very sweet teacher lady. Her first semester teaching 276 i believe and she did okay. Only thing was she didn't put our grades online ALL semester and will not respond to emails so you have no clue what your grade is. Also her study guides are useless. There is way too much information on them to study effectively. Overall, the class was simply ok.

She is by far the worst teacher I have had.  This class was supposed to be a business statistics class, but she taught it as if it were a psych stats class.  I did less than half the HW and in-class assignments, got A's on all the tests and got a B.  This class is a joke and she is a joke of a teacher as far as MGMT 276 goes!!!

Good teacher, boring topic.  Information was presented logically and step by step.  Go to class and the course is a breeze.  From what I've heard about the old 276 teachers Delaney is 1000% percent better

THE WORST TEACHER EVER. SHE CANNOT TEACH TO SAVE HER LIFE. SHE CRACKS USELESS JOKES AND WASTES OUR TIME. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS WITH HER. HER TESTS ARE HORRIBLE. SHES JUST PATHETIC AND NEEDS TO STOP TEACHER. PEOPLE KEEP SAYING SHES GREAT AT PSYCH AND SHE MIGHT BE BUT SHE DEFINITELY SHOULDN'T TEACH MGMT.

This is by far the most boring, un-engaging class I have taken at the university.  The exams are a mess and poorly worded.  She often has trouble explaining concepts because she gets them confused in her own head!  Do yourself a favor and take someone else for this class.
great teacher

Wow.  Words cannot begin to describe how useless she is as a MGMT 276 teacher.  Why would they have this psychology teacher lecture statistics? The Texts book has little to nothing to do with the exams or material taught in class.  She choose to include another text ?The Psychology of Judgment and Decision Making? which is simply a book about vari

Awesome teacher!  Glad we got her from Psych.  She is very personable and interacts comfortably with class. Always provides sufficient examples and plenty of review of the concepts.  Truly one of the best EDUCATORS at the U!
She is a really good professor. She makes learning really fun and easy. She is also pretty funny. Exams were also open notes so the class was supper easy.

She was a great teacher. She is more concept-based and doesn't make you learn how to do the math for everything which makes focusing on the actual material much easier. She is such a sweet lady, I can't emphasize enough. Also extremely understanding of any problems or struggles you have with the material. I recommend strongly!!!

Great teacher. Her lectures can be boring and you're in trouble if you miss more than a few but she will help you all day in office hours if you need it. She teaches a difficult subject but makes things easier to understand. I recommend her to anyone who asks!

Delaney has a unique ability to keep students interested in the material. Content is super boring but relevant and applicable in any real-life setting. The first two tests were SLIGHTLY tougher than the last two but honestly I studied two days before and still got As. Go to class for attendance points and do the homework and you'll be golden :)
Dr. Delaney is a terrific teacher. If you go to class, take notes, and pay attention you won't have problems. Highly recommended for Mgmt 276.

Requires 110% effort. If you don't get it you won't get it unless you spend the time to learn it and it will most definitely be outside of lectures. Lectures and slides are confusing and are not great materials to review from.

She treated the class like we were five, her slides had animations and music on them, but overall not a bad teacher. You learn the material through her lectures as well as the homework. Go to the review thats offered. That's pretty  much all I studied. I got an A without buying the book, but do the Plous reading and go to class for points.

the class can be difficult if you do not study. but the info is actuall pretty clear cut. before the tests go through the homework and slides more than just one time youll do fine. first test didn't study got an F, studied the other ones got As. do the homework and jut go to class even though lectures suck.

She's super nice, very willing to re-explain concepts, and very practical. She always makes sure you know WHY you're learning what you are. The class is super easy, don't expect math. It's sort of how statistics works, not why it works, very general. If you are an Econ major, take ECON339 instead because while she is great, you'll be unprepared.

Overall, a pretty dry class; however, Dr. Delaney is a very knowledgeable and caring professor.  The tests are challenging but put some time and effort into them and you will do just fine. Don't buy the textbook, everything is online. The last two tests were easier than the first two

Enthusiastic, helpful, and fun teacher! Boring subject but she makes it interesting. Her assignments are easy and tests are easy if you make sure to understand everything in her study guides. Assigned seating sucks but it does help to improve focus and attendance. Don't buy the book. Anyone can get an A in this class!
Attendance is extra credit. She's actually really funny and will always let you out of class early. She's sweet too.

There is a lot of homework and in class assignments but you could literally write anything on them and still get credit for them. But for your overall grade they dont even count for much. Her lectures aren't very helpful because she spends more time talking about the slide animations than the actual material. Very nice lady though lol

Annoyingly strict on attendance and in class participation(15% of grade). This wouldn't be a problem if she was a better and more engaging teacher. It was easy to miss class and earn A's on the exams. Offers little to no extra credit.

Got a 79%, only because I lost my homework and the tests are based on that. She didn't post any keys for the HW, and about 20% of every test is from HW's that she doesn't have answers for. Lectures are boring af. MY ONLY C AT THE UNIVERSITY!
